titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Joseph Brady to Edward O'Farrell, 3 June 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Joseph Brady

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Patrick Joseph Brady (1868–1943) an Irish solicitor and politician, to Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), assistant under-secretary for Ireland. The letter refers to the detention of Otto Krautwurst (b.1878), a German professor who is being held in Oldcastle POW camp for being and illegal alien. It states that he is in poor health and that this should be grounds for his release.Patrick Joseph Brady represented the Stephen’s Green division for the Irish Parliamentary Party in Westminster from 1910-1918. Professor Krautwurst was under surveillance since April 1910 by the DMP. He had committed no crime but had purchased “a substantial dwelling facing the sea,' a house in Sandymount at 17 Strand Road.

msIdentifier.collection: National Archive of Ireland-Chief Secretary's Office-Registered Papers, NAI/CSO/RP/1916/5612/9487/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
'PHONE 82, BLACKROCK. GLENA, BOOTERSTOWN, Co. DUBLIN 3rd June 1916. Office Sir Edward O'Farrell, Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle. Dear Sir Edward O'Farrell, Mr Stephenson, of the firm of Messrs
O'Keeffe & Lynch, writes to me urging the release of Mr Kranhourst
at present interned in Old castle as an alien. It appears that
he is in a precarious state of health and has been so for some
time. I hope it will be possible to santion his release. Yours faithfully,
Patrick J. Brady. Assistant Secretary
please see letter to the
Home office on the 11 th April
and Police report of the 7th
Inst.My speculation is that
the question of health wasfully
dealt with on papers sent
to HD.W M L 8/6 Inform M Brady
firm mssrs O'Keefe & Lynch
make any representation
in the forward, health
will be considered but
there is no present
intention of releasing
this man
EOF 8.6.16 Other signatures at the bottom Don E H 9/6
denoy 9/6
